posts.text: The case for on-chain property registries is straightforward: cryptographic proofs are cheaper and more reliable than paper-based county systems. Verifiable ownership records make title insurance obsolete.

This is the right framing. Digital identity of an asset must be separate from the identity of its physical owner. That separation is what makes privacy-preserving tokenization of real estate possible.

references.author: Crypto Punk
references.text: If we tokenize real estate, the digital identity of the asset and the physical owner must be separated. Otherwise you are doxxing every property owner on the blockchain, which is a non-starter for adoption.
